author: exe.dev user
text: Reduced from ~155ms to ~125ms by mocking articles.js, api.js, and modal.js, plus replacing vi.waitFor with flushPromises. The remaining ~125ms is dominated by per-test happy-dom DOM parsing (~2ms/test × 60 tests). Reliably getting below 100ms would require either (a) reducing the number of tests by consolidating similar ones, (b) sharing DOM setup across tests via describe-level beforeEach (difficult because forms differ), or (c) using a faster DOM engine. The <100ms target may be unrealistic for 60 DOM-heavy tests in happy-dom.

author: exe.dev user
text: Apr 13 update: skarredghost.com/feed/ (feeds 197, 589) now also showing HTTP 409 Conflict errors — two separate user subscriptions to the same URL. Same no-backoff root cause. The 409 hits the default 4xx description path in httpErrorDescription().
